content: I sent you new app image for sisyphus. Can you update the service?

input.command: find / -name "*.AppImage" -newer /tmp 2>/dev/null | head -20; systemctl status sisyphus 2>/dev/null | head -20

content.content:
● sisyphus.service - Sisyphus Agent Orchestrator
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/sisyphus.service; enabled; preset: disabled)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: active (running) since Wed 2026-04-15 22:36:34 EDT; 4 days ago
 Invocation: 8d1e4a31c10a454d9a69e0273d52dc16
   Main PID: 3506184 (sisyphus)
      Tasks: 13 (limit: 19036)
     Memory: 131.9M (peak: 806.2M)
        CPU: 14.684s
     CGroup: /system.slice/sisyphus.service
             ├─3506184 /tmp/.mount_sisyphLalPfL/usr/bin/sisyphus
             └─3506247 /usr/local/bin/sisyphus

[Unit]
Description=Sisyphus Agent Orchestrator
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/sisyphus
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
User=patronum
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/patronum

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 391551480 Apr 15 22:36 /usr/local/bin/sisyphus

input.command: ls -la /home/danny/ 2>/dev/null; ls -la /root/ 2>/dev/null | grep -i sisy; ls -la /tmp/ | grep -i sisy

content.text: I can see the binary is at `/usr/local/bin/sisyphus` — but I can't find the new AppImage you sent. Where did you drop it?
